fields.name: RESERVED
fields.description: reserved for future use

fields.name: RCV_OFF
fields.description: analog part of the receiver is switched off

enum_values.1: analog part of the receiver is switched off

fields.name: POWER_DOWN
fields.description: Soft power-down mode entered

enum_values.0: MFRC522 starts the wake up procedure

fields.name: COMMAND
fields.description: activates a command based on the Command value

Command and Status.name: COM_IEN_REG
Command and Status.description: Control bits to enable and disable the passing of interrupt requests

fields.name: IRQ_INV
fields.description: signal on pin IRQ is inverted with respect to the Status1Reg register's IRq bit

enum_values.1: signal on pin IRQ is inverted
enum_values.0: signal on pin IRQ is equal to the IRq bit

fields.name: TX_IEN
fields.description: allows the transmitter interrupt request (TxIRq bit) to be propagated to pin IRQ

fields.name: RX_IEN
fields.description: allows the receiver interrupt request (RxIRq bit) to be propagated to pin IRQ

fields.name: IDLE_IEN
fields.description: allows the idle interrupt request (IdleIRq bit) to be propagated to pin IRQ

fields.name: HI_ALERT_IEN
fields.description: allows the high alert interrupt request (HiAlertIRq bit) to be propagated to pin IRQ

fields.name: LO_ALERT_IEN
fields.description: allows the low alert interrupt request (LoAlertIRq bit) to be propagated to pin IRQ

fields.name: ERR_IEN
fields.description: allows the error interrupt request (ErrIRq bit) to be propagated to pin IRQ

fields.name: TIMER_IEN
fields.description: allows the timer interrupt request (TimerIRq bit) to be propagated to pin IRQ

Command and Status.name: DIV_IEN_REG
Command and Status.description: Control bits to enable and disable the passing of interrupt requests.

fields.name: IRQ_PUSH_PULL
fields.description: pin IRQ is a standard CMOS output pin when 1, open-drain when 0

fields.name: MFIN_ACT_IEN
fields.description: allows the MFIN active interrupt request to be propagated to pin IRQ

fields.name: CRC_IEN
fields.description: allows the CRC interrupt request to be propagated to pin IRQ

fields.name: RSVD_1_0
fields.description: Reserved

fields.name: RSVD_3
fields.description: Reserved

fields.name: RSVD_6_5
fields.description: Reserved

Command and Status.name: COM_IRQ_REG
Command and Status.description: Interrupt request bits. All bits cleared by software.

fields.name: SET1
fields.description: Set (1) or clear (0) marked bits in ComIrqReg

fields.name: TX_IRQ
fields.description: set after last bit of transmitted data sent

fields.name: RX_IRQ
fields.description: receiver detected end of valid data stream

fields.name: IDLE_IRQ
fields.description: set when command terminates to Idle state

fields.name: HI_ALERT_IRQ
fields.description: HiAlert event latched from Status1Reg

fields.name: LO_ALERT_IRQ
fields.description: LoAlert event latched from Status1Reg

fields.name: ERR_IRQ
fields.description: any error bit in ErrorReg is set

fields.name: TIMER_IRQ
fields.description: timer decremented to zero

Command and Status.name: ERROR_REG
Command and Status.description: Error bit register showing error status of last command executed.

fields.name: WR_ERR
fields.description: Write error to FIFO during MFAuthent or TX/RX

fields.name: TEMP_ERR
fields.description: Temperature sensor detects overheating

fields.name: RESERVED
fields.description: reserved for future use

fields.name: BUFFER_OVFL
fields.description: FIFO buffer overflow

fields.name: COLL_ERR
fields.description: Bit collision detected

fields.name: CRC_ERR
fields.description: CRC calculation failed

fields.name: PARITY_ERR
fields.description: Parity check failed

fields.name: PROTOCOL_ERR
fields.description: SOF incorrect or MFAuthent byte count wrong

Command and Status.name: STATUS1_REG
Command and Status.description: Contains status bits of the CRC, interrupt and FIFO buffer.

fields.name: RESERVED
fields.description: reserved for future use

fields.name: CRCOk
fields.description: the CRC result is zero for data transmission and reception, the CRCOk bit is undefined: use the ErrorReg register's CRCErr bit indicates the status of the CRCcoprocessor, during calculation the value changes to logic 0, when the calculation is done correctly the value changes to logic 1

fields.name: CRCReady
fields.description: the CRC calculation has finished only valid for the CRC coprocessor calculation using the CalcCRC command

fields.name: IRq
fields.description: indicates if any interrupt source requests attention with respect to the setting of the interrupt enable bits: see the ComIEnReg and DivIEnReg registers

fields.name: TRunning
fields.description: MFRC522's timer unit is running, i.e. the timer will decrement the TCounterValReg register with the next timer clock Remark: in gated mode, the TRunning bit is set to logic 1 when the timer is enabled by TModeReg register's TGated[1:0] bits; this bit is not influenced by the gated signal

fields.name: HiAlert
fields.description: the number of bytes stored in the FIFO buffer corresponds to equation: example: FIFO length = 60, WaterLevel = 4 → HiAlert = 1 FIFO length = 59, WaterLevel = 4 → HiAlert = 0 HiAlert ≤ (64 - FIFOLength) WaterLevel =

fields.name: LoAlert
fields.description: the number of bytes stored in the FIFO buffer corresponds to equation: example: FIFO length = 4, WaterLevel = 4 → LoAlert = 1 FIFO length = 5, WaterLevel = 4 → LoAlert = 0 LoAlert ≤ FIFOLength WaterLevel =

fields.name: RSVD_2
fields.description: Reserved

Command and Status.name: STATUS2_REG
Command and Status.description: Contains status bits of the receiver, transmitter and data mode detector.

fields.name: TEMPSENSCLEAR
fields.description: clears the temperature error if the temperature is below the alarm limit of 125 °C

fields.name: I2CFORCEHS
fields.description: I^2C-bus input filter settings: 1 = High-speed mode independent of the I^2C-bus protocol, 0 = set to the I^2C-bus protocol used

fields.name: RESERVED
fields.description: reserved

fields.name: MFCRYPTO1ON
fields.description: indicates that the MIFARE Crypto1 unit is switched on and therefore all data communication with the card is encrypted

fields.name: MODEMSTATE
fields.description: shows the state of the transmitter and receiver state machines

enum_values.0b001: wait for the BitFramingReg register's StartSend bit
enum_values.0b010: TxWait: wait until RF field is present
enum_values.0b100: RxWait: wait until RF field is present

FIFO.name: FIFO_DATA_REG
FIFO.description: Input and output of 64 byte FIFO buffer.

fields.name: FIFO_DATA
fields.description: data input and output port for the internal 64-byte FIFO buffer

FIFO.name: FIFO_LEVEL_REG
FIFO.description: FIFO level register with FlushBuffer and FIFOLevel[6:0]

fields.name: FLUSH_BUFFER
fields.description: Reset FIFO buffer pointers

fields.name: FIFO_LEVEL
fields.description: Number of bytes stored in FIFO buffer

FIFO.name: WATER_LEVEL_REG
FIFO.description: Defines the level for FIFO under- and overflow warning.

fields.name: RESERVED
fields.description: reserved for future use

fields.name: WATER_LEVEL
fields.description: defines a warning level to indicate a FIFO buffer overflow or underflow

TX Control.name: TXMODEREG
TX Control.description: TX mode register controlling data rate and framing during transmission

TX Control.name: TX_MODE_REG
TX Control.description: Defines the data rate during transmission.

fields.name: TX_CRC_EN
fields.description: enables CRC generation during data transmission Remark: can only be set to logic 0 at 106 kBd

fields.name: TX_SPEED
fields.description: defines the bit rate during data transmission the MFRC522 handles transfer speeds up to 848 kBd

fields.name: INV_MOD
fields.description: modulation of transmitted data is inverted

fields.name: RESERVED
fields.description: reserved for future use

TX Control.name: TX_CONTROL_REG
TX Control.description: Controls the logical behavior of the antenna driver pins TX1 and TX2.

fields.name: INV_TX2_RF_ON
fields.description: output signal on pin TX2 inverted when driver TX2 is enabled

fields.name: INV_TX1_RF_ON
fields.description: output signal on pin TX1 inverted when driver TX1 is enabled

fields.name: INV_TX2_RF_OFF
fields.description: output signal on pin TX2 inverted when driver TX2 is disabled

fields.name: INV_TX1_RF_OFF
fields.description: output signal on pin TX1 inverted when driver TX1 is disabled

fields.name: TX2_CW
fields.description: output signal on pin TX2 continuously delivers the unmodulated 13.56 MHz energy carrier

fields.name: RESERVED
fields.description: reserved for future use

fields.name: TX2_RF_EN
fields.description: output signal on pin TX2 delivers the 13.56 MHz energy carrier modulated by the transmission data

fields.name: TX1_RF_EN
fields.description: output signal on pin TX1 delivers the 13.56 MHz energy carrier modulated by the transmission data

fields.name: RESERVED_7
fields.description: reserved for future use

fields.name: FORCE_100_ASK
fields.description: forces a 100 %ASK modulation independent of the ModGsPReg register setting

fields.name: RESERVED_5_0
fields.description: reserved for future use

TX Control.name: TX_SEL
TX Control.description: Selects the internal sources for the analog module.

fields.name: RESERVED
fields.description: reserved for future use

fields.name: DRIVER_SEL
fields.description: selects the input of drivers TX1 and TX2

enum_values.0b00: 3-state; in soft power-down the drivers are only in 3-state mode if the DriverSel[1:0] value is set to 3-state mode
enum_values.0b01: modulation signal (envelope) from the internal encoder, Miller pulse encoded
enum_values.0b11: HIGH; the HIGH level depends on the setting of bits InvTx1RFOn/InvTx1RFOff and InvTx2RFOn/InvTx2RFOff

fields.name: MFOUT_SEL
fields.description: selects the input for pin MFOUT

enum_values.0b0011: test bus signal as defined by the TestSel1Reg register's TstBusBitSel[2:0] value
enum_values.0b0100: modulation signal (envelope) from the internal encoder, Miller pulse encoded
enum_values.0b0101: serial data stream to be transmitted, data stream before Miller encoder
enum_values.0b0111: serial data stream received, data stream after Manchester decoder

fields.name: MOD_WIDTH
fields.description: defines the width of the Miller modulation as multiples of the carrier frequency (ModWidth + 1 / f_clk ) the maximum value is half the bit period

TX Control.name: GsNReg
TX Control.description: Defines the conductance of the antenna driver pins TX1 and TX2 for the n-driver when the driver is switched on.

fields.name: CWGsN
fields.description: defines the conductance of the output n-driver during periods without modulation which can be used to regulate the output power and subsequently current consumption and operating distance Remark: the conductance value is binary-weighted during soft Power-down mode the highest bit is forced to logic 1 value is only used if driver TX1 or TX2 is switched on

fields.name: ModGsN
fields.description: defines the conductance of the output n-driver during periods without modulation which can be used to regulate the modulation index Remark: the conductance value is binary weighted during soft Power-down mode the highest bit is forced to logic 1 value is only used if driver TX1 or TX2 is switched on

TX Control.name: CWGsPReg
TX Control.description: Defines the conductance of the p-driver output during periods of no modulation

fields.name: reserved
fields.description: reserved for future use

fields.name: CWGsP
fields.description: defines the conductance of the p-driver output which can be used to regulate the output power and subsequently current consumption and operating distance Remark: the conductance value is binary weighted during soft Power-down mode the highest bit is forced to logic 1

TX Control.name: MOD_GSP_REG
TX Control.description: Defines the conductance of the p-driver output during modulation.

fields.name: RESERVED
fields.description: reserved for future use

fields.name: MOD_GSP
fields.description: defines the conductance of the p-driver output during modulation which can be used to regulate the modulation index

RX Control.name: RX_MODE_REG
RX Control.description: Defines the data rate during reception.

fields.name: RX_CRC_EN
fields.description: enables the CRC calculation during reception Remark: can only be set to logic 0 at 106 kBd

fields.name: RX_SPEED
fields.description: defines the bit rate while receiving data the MFRC522 handles transfer speeds up to 848 kBd

fields.name: RX_NO_ERR
fields.description: an invalid received data stream (less than 4 bits received) will be ignored and the receiver remains active

fields.name: RX_MULTIPLE
fields.description: able to receive more than one data frame only valid for data rates above 106 kBd

fields.name: RESERVED
fields.description: reserved for future use

fields.name: UART_SEL
fields.description: selects the input of the contactless UART

enum_values.0b01: Manchester with subcarrier from pin MFIN
enum_values.0b10: modulated signal from the internal analog module, default
enum_values.0b11: NRZ coding without subcarrier from pin MFIN which is only valid for transfer speeds above 106 kBd

fields.name: RX_WAIT
fields.description: after data transmission the activation of the receiver is delayed for RxWait bit-clocks, during this 'frame guard time' any signal on pin RX is ignored

RX Control.name: RX_THRESHOLD_REG
RX Control.description: selects thresholds for the bit decoder

RX Control.name: RX_THRESHOLD
RX Control.description: Selects thresholds for the bit decoder.

fields.name: MIN_LEVEL
fields.description: defines the minimum signal strength at the decoder input that will be accepted

fields.name: RESERVED
fields.description: reserved for future use

fields.name: COLL_LEVEL
fields.description: defines the minimum signal strength at the decoder input that must be reached by the weaker half-bit of the Manchester encoded signal to generate a bit-collision

fields.name: ADD_IQ
fields.description: defines the use of I and Q channel during reception Remark: the FixIQ bit must be set to logic 0 to enable the following settings:

enum_values.0b01: selects the stronger channel and freezes the selected channel during communication

fields.name: FIX_IQ
fields.description: if AddIQ[1:0] are set to X0b, the reception is fixed to I channel if AddIQ[1:0] are set to X1b, the reception is fixed to Q channel

fields.name: TPRESCAL_EVEN
fields.description: Available on RC522 version 1.0 and version 2.0: If set to logic 0 the following formula is used to calculate the timer frequency of the prescaler: f_timer = 13.56 MHz / (2*TPreScaler+1). Only available on version 2.0: If set to logic 1 the following formula is used to calculate the timer frequency of the prescaler: f_timer = 13.56 MHz / (2*TPreScaler+2). Default TPrescalEven bit is logic 0, find more information on the prescaler in Section 8.5.

fields.name: TAU_RCV
fields.description: changes the time-constant of the internal PLL during data reception Remark: if set to 00b the PLL is frozen during data reception

fields.name: TAU_SYNC
fields.description: changes the time-constant of the internal PLL during burst

fields.name: RESERVED
fields.description: reserved for future use

fields.name: TX_WAIT
fields.description: Additional response time: 7 bits added to register value

fields.name: RESERVED
fields.description: reserved for future use

fields.name: ParityDisable
fields.description: generation of the parity bit for transmission and the parity check for receiving is switched off the received parity bit is handled like a data bit

fields.name: RESERVED_3_0
fields.description: reserved for future use

Timer.name: TMODEREG
Timer.description: Defines the higher 4 bits of the TPrescaler value and timer mode settings.

fields.name: TAUTO
fields.description: timer starts automatically at the end of the transmission in all communication modes at all speeds

enum_values.0: timer is not influenced by the protocol

fields.name: TGATED
fields.description: internal timer is running in gated mode

fields.name: TAUTO_RESTART
fields.description: timer automatically restarts its count-down from the 16-bit timer reload value instead of counting down to zero

enum_values.0: timer decrements to 0 and the ComIrqReg register's TimerIRq bit is set to logic 1

fields.name: TPRESCALER_HI
fields.description: defines the higher 4 bits of the TPrescaler value

fields.name: T_PREScaler_LO
fields.description: Timer prescaler low 8 bits

Timer.name: T_RELOAD_REG_HI
Timer.description: Timer reload register high byte TReloadVal_Hi[7:0]

fields.name: T_RELOAD_VAL_HI
fields.description: Timer reload value high byte

fields.name: TRELOAD_VAL_HI
fields.description: defines the higher 8 bits of the 16-bit timer reload value on a start event, the timer loads the timer reload value changing this register affects the timer only at the next start event

Timer.name: T_RELOAD_REG_LO
Timer.description: Timer reload register low byte TReloadVal_Lo[7:0]

fields.name: T_RELOAD_VAL_LO
fields.description: Timer reload value low byte

fields.name: TRELOAD_VAL_LO
fields.description: defines the lower 8 bits of the 16-bit timer reload value on a start event, the timer loads the timer reload value changing this register affects the timer only at the next start event

Timer.name: T_COUNTER_VAL_REG_HI
Timer.description: Timer counter value register high byte

fields.name: T_COUNTER_VAL_HI
fields.description: Current timer value high byte

fields.name: TCOUNTER_VAL_HI
fields.description: timer value higher 8 bits

Timer.name: T_COUNTER_VAL_REG_LO
Timer.description: Timer counter value register low byte

fields.name: T_COUNTER_VAL_LO
fields.description: Current timer value low byte

fields.name: TCOUNTER_VAL_LO
fields.description: timer value lower 8 bits

fields.name: CRC_RESULT_MSB
fields.description: CRC result MSB[7:0]

fields.name: CRCRESULT_MSB
fields.description: shows the value of the CRCResultReg register's most significant byte only valid if Status1Reg register's CRCReady bit is set to logic 1

fields.name: CRCRESULT_LSB
fields.description: shows the value of the least significant byte of the CRCResultReg register only valid if Status1Reg register's CRCReady bit is set to logic 1

fields.name: RESERVED
fields.description: reserved for future use

fields.name: TST_BUS_BIT_SEL
fields.description: selects a test bus signal which is output at pin MFOUT if AnalogSelAux2[3:0] = FFh in AnalogTestReg register, test bus signal is also output at pins AUX1 or AUX2

Test and Debug.name: TEST_SEL2
Test and Debug.description: General test signal configuration and PRBS control.

fields.name: TST_BUS_FLIP
fields.description: test bus is mapped to the parallel port in the following order: TstBusBit4,TstBusBit3, TstBusBit2, TstBusBit6, TstBusBit5, TstBusBit0

enum_values.1: test bus is mapped to the parallel port in the following order: TstBusBit4,TstBusBit3, TstBusBit2, TstBusBit6, TstBusBit5, TstBusBit0

fields.name: PRBS9
fields.description: starts and enables the PRBS9 sequence according to ITU-TO150

fields.name: PRBS15
fields.description: starts and enables the PRBS15 sequence according to ITU-TO150

fields.name: TEST_BUS_SEL
fields.description: selects the test bus

Test and Debug.name: TEST_PIN_EN
Test and Debug.description: Enables the test bus pin output driver.

fields.name: RS232_LINE_EN
fields.description: serial UART lines MX and DTRQ are disabled

enum_values.0: serial UART lines MX and DTRQ are disabled

fields.name: TEST_PIN_EN
fields.description: enables the output driver on one of the data pins D1 to D7 which outputs a test signal

fields.name: RESERVED
fields.description: reserved for future use

Test and Debug.name: TEST_PIN_VALUE
Test and Debug.description: Defines the HIGH and LOW values for the test port D1 to D7 when it is used as I/O.

fields.name: USE_IO
fields.description: enables the I/O functionality for the test port

enum_values.1: enables the I/O functionality for the test port

fields.name: TEST_PIN_VALUE
fields.description: defines the value of the test port when it is used as I/O

fields.name: RESERVED
fields.description: reserved for future use

Test and Debug.name: TEST_BUS
Test and Debug.description: Shows the status of the internal test bus.

fields.name: TEST_BUS
fields.description: shows the status of the internal test bus

fields.name: RESERVED
fields.description: reserved for production tests

fields.name: AMP_RCV
fields.description: internal signal processing in the receiver chain is performed non-linearly which increases the operating distance in communication modes at 106 kBd Remark: due to non-linearity, the effect of the RxThresholdReg register's MinLevel[3:0] and the CollLevel[2:0] values is also non-linear

enum_values.1: internal signal processing in the receiver chain is performed non-linearly which increases the operating distance in communication modes at 106 kBd Remark: due to non-linearity, the effect of the RxThresholdReg register's MinLevel[3:0] and the CollLevel[2:0] values is also non-linear

fields.name: RFT
fields.description: reserved for production tests

fields.name: SELF_TEST
fields.description: enables the digital self test the self test can also be started by the CalcCRC command; see Section 10.3.1.4 on page 71 the self test is enabled by value 1001b Remark: for default operation the self test must be disabled by value 0000b

fields.name: CHIPTYPE
fields.description: '9' stands for MFRC522

fields.name: VERSION
fields.description: '1' stands for MFRC522 version 1.0 and '2' stands for MFRC522 version 2.0.

Test and Debug.name: ANALOG_TEST_REG
Test and Debug.description: Determines the analog output test signal at, and status of, pins AUX1 and AUX2.

fields.name: ANALOG_SEL_AUX1
fields.description: controls pin AUX1 3-state

fields.name: ANALOG_SEL_AUX2
fields.description: controls pin AUX2 (see bit descriptions for AUX1)

Test and Debug.name: TEST_DAC1_REG
Test and Debug.description: Defines the test value for TestDAC1.

fields.name: RESERVED
fields.description: reserved for production tests and future use

fields.name: TEST_DAC1
fields.description: defines the test value for TestDAC1 output of DAC1 can be routed to AUX1 by setting value AnalogSelAux1[3:0] to 0001b in the AnalogTestReg register

Test and Debug.name: TEST_DAC2_REG
Test and Debug.description: Defines the test value for TestDAC2.

fields.name: RESERVED
fields.description: reserved for future use

fields.name: TEST_DAC2
fields.description: defines the test value for TestDAC2

Test and Debug.name: TEST_ADC_REG
Test and Debug.description: Shows the values of ADC I and Q channels.

fields.name: ADC_I
fields.description: ADC I channel value

fields.name: ADC_Q
fields.description: ADC Q channel value

fields.name: T_STOP_NOW
fields.description: timer stops immediately reading this bit always returns it to logic0

fields.name: T_START_NOW
fields.description: timer starts immediately reading this bit always returns it to logic 0

fields.name: RESERVED
fields.description: reserved for future use

fields.name: RX_LAST_BITS
fields.description: indicates the number of valid bits in the last received byte if this value is 000b, the whole byte is valid

fields.name: START_SEND
fields.description: starts the transmission of data only valid in combination with the Transceive command

fields.name: RX_ALIGN
fields.description: used for reception of bit-oriented frames: defines the bit position for the first bit received to be stored in the FIFO buffer

fields.name: RESERVED
fields.description: reserved for future use

fields.name: TX_LAST_BITS
fields.description: used for transmission of bit oriented frames: defines the number of bits of the last byte that will be transmitted 000b indicates that all bits of the last byte will be transmitted

Control and Mode.name: COLL_REG
Control and Mode.description: Defines the first bit-collision detected on the RF interface.

fields.name: VALUES_AFTER_COLL
fields.description: all received bits will be cleared after a collision only used during bitwise anticollision at 106 kBd, otherwise it is set to logic 1

fields.name: RESERVED
fields.description: reserved for future use

fields.name: COLL_POS_NOT_VALID
fields.description: no collision detected or the position of the collision is out of the range of CollPos[4:0]

fields.name: COLL_POS
fields.description: shows the bit position of the first detected collision in a received frame only data bits are interpreted

Control and Mode.name: MODE_REG
Control and Mode.description: Defines general mode settings for transmitting and receiving.

fields.name: MSB_FIRST
fields.description: CRC coprocessor calculates the CRC with MSB first in the CRCResultReg register the values for the CRCResultMSB[7:0] bits and the CRCResultLSB[7:0] bits are bit reversed

fields.name: RESERVED_6
fields.description: reserved for future use

fields.name: TX_WAIT_RF
fields.description: transmitter can only be started if an RF field is generated

fields.name: RESERVED_4
fields.description: reserved for future use

fields.name: POL_MFIN
fields.description: defines the polarity of pin MFIN

fields.name: RESERVED_2
fields.description: reserved for future use

fields.name: CRC_PRESET
fields.description: defines the preset value for the CRCcoprocessor for the CalcCRC command

RF Framing.name: SERIAL_SPEED_REG
RF Framing.description: Sets UART transfer speed using BR_T0[2:0] and BR_T1[4:0] factors

fields.name: BR_T1
fields.description: BR_T1[4:0] factor for UART baud rate

fields.name: BR_T0
fields.description: BR_T0[2:0] factor for UART baud rate

fields.name: RSVD_7_1
fields.description: Reserved

fields.name: Set2
fields.description: indicates that the marked bits in the DivIrqReg register are set (1) / cleared (0)

enum_values.1: indicates that the marked bits in the DivIrqReg register are set
enum_values.0: indicates that the marked bits in the DivIrqReg register are cleared

fields.name: reserved
fields.description: reserved for future use
